data.title: Bavarian Nordic A/S reports interim Q1 results
data.description:
• Bavarian Nordic A/S press release (OTCPK:BVNKF): Q1 revenue increased by 62% to DKK 1,347M.
  • Operating profit (EBITDA) was DKK 420M, corresponding to an EBITDA margin of 31%.
  • Financial guidance for the full year is maintained at a revenue of DKK 5,700M-DKK 6,700M and an EBITDA margin of 26-30%.

data.title: Nippon Telegraph and Telephone reports FY results
data.description:
• Nippon Telegraph and Telephone Corporation press release (OTCPK:NTTYY): FY GAAP EPS (basic) of ¥11.96.

  • Operating Revenues of ¥13,704.7B (+2.5% Y/Y).

data.title: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries reports FY results
data.description:
• Mitsubishi Heavy Industries press release (OTCPK:MHVYF): FY profit attributable to owners of parent (net income) was ¥245.4B, an increase of 10.6% year-on-year, with a profit margin of 4.9%.
  • Revenue rose 7.9% year-on-year to ¥5,027.1 billion.
  • EBITDA was ¥541.3B, a 25.1% increase over FY2023, with an EBITDA margin of 10.8%, up 1.5 percentage points year-on-year.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: PINS, AFRM, and more
data.description:
Stock futures were mixed Friday premarket as investors digested the newly announced U.S.-U.K. trade agreement, hoping it signals broader progress ahead.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Pinterest (NYSE:PINS) +14% – Shares surged after the company beat Q1 expectations, with revenue up 16% and global MAUs rising 10%. For Q2, Pinterest guided revenue of $960M–$980M (midpoint $970M vs. $963.7M est.), implying 12%–15% Y/Y growth, and projected adjusted EBITDA of $217M–$237M.
  • Cloudflare (NYSE:NET) +10% – Shares rose after the cybersecurity and content delivery firm posted mixed Q1 results, with revenue up 27% Y/Y to $479.1M, surpassing the $470M consensus. For Q2, Cloudflare guided for revenue of $500M–$501M, broadly in line with the $500.9M estimate, and adjusted EPS of $0.18, just below the $0.19 consensus. The company reaffirmed its full-year forecast, expecting revenue of $2.09B and EPS between $0.79 and $0.80, both slightly below the consensus of $2.09B and $0.82 per share.
  • Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) +7% – Shares gained after the company reported a 14% Y/Y revenue rise to $1.45B in Q1, driven by a record 218.4M rides and an all-time high in driver hours. Active riders rose 11% to 24.2M, and adjusted EBITDA nearly doubled to $106.5M, with a 100bps margin improvement to 2.6%. Sentiment was further boosted by Lyft’s expanded $750M buyback program, with $500M set for deployment over the next year—$200M in the next three months—representing 13–14% of its market cap. For Q2, Lyft expects gross bookings of $4.41B–$4.57B (+10–14% Y/Y) and adjusted EBITDA of $115M–$130M with a margin of 2.6–2.8%.

Biggest stock losers

  • Affirm (NASDAQ:AFRM) -7% – Buy now pay later stock declined after CEO Max Levchin acknowledged that the company’s 0% APR installment loans generate lower revenue than other products, though the stronger credit quality offsets the margin trade-off. He also flagged potential macro risks. Still, the company raised its FY2025 gross merchandise volume outlook to $35.7B–$36.0B (vs. $35.3B est.) and revenue in the range of $3.163B–$3.193B (vs. $3.18B est.). Affirm also tightened transaction cost guidance to $1.721B–$1.736B and improved its adjusted operating margin forecast to 23%–23.6%. For Q4, AFRM projects $815M–$845M in revenue, above the consensus of $841.6M, and $9.4B–$9.7B in GMV, above the Visible Alpha estimate of $9.27B.

data.title: Alpha Bank reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Alpha Bank press release (OTCPK:ALBKY): Q1 reported profit after income tax of €223.3M (+5.2% Y/Y).
  • Net Interest Income of €395.3M (-6.2% Y/Y).
  • Core PPI stood at €307.8M (-4.9% Y/Y).
  • Tangible book value per share of €3.07.

data.title: Commerzbank AG reports Q1 results; reaffirms FY outlook
data.description:
• Commerzbank AG press release (OTCPK:CRZBF): Q1 Cost-income ratio reduced by 2 percentage points to 56% – below target of 57% for full year.
  • Revenue of €3.07B (+11.6% Y/Y).
  • Double-digit return on tangible equity (Net RoTE) of 11.1% achieved.
  • CET 1 ratio of 15.1% demonstrates high potential for capital return – application for next share buyback targeted for beginning of third quarter.
  • Outlook for full year 2025 confirmed – CET 1 ratio of at least 14.5% expected

  • Commerzbank continues to aim for a higher net result of around €2.8bn for the full year, before restructuring expenses. After restructuring expenses, it expects a result of around €2.4bn. The outlook remains subject to the development of burdens related to Russia and foreign currency loans at mBank. The Bank anticipates a net interest income of around €7.8bn, along with a related positive fair value adjustment of approximately €0.3bn. In total, Commerzbank expects a contribution of around €8.1bn to revenues. It plans for a growth of around 7% in net commission income and confirms its cost-income ratio target of around 57%. Despite the challenging economic environment, it expects the risk result to remain at around minus €850m, assuming usage of TLA. Commerzbank anticipates a CET 1 ratio of at least 14.5% by year-end after the planned capital return and restructuring expenses. In February of this year, the Bank had expected more than 14%.

    The Bank confirms its plans for capital return: for the financial year 2025, it aims to return 100% of net income before restructuring expenses and after deduction of AT1 coupon payments to its shareholders.
